items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino is reaching out to shopping centers with a new amnesty program to help businesses get properly licensed. The city is also rolling out incentive programs for retail, small businesses, and startups as part of economic development efforts.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield
items.summary: Mountain View approved a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road through a development agreement. This project will add new housing to the area, though specific details about unit count and design were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city applied for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, which provides state funding to cities that remove housing barriers. This suggests Mountain View is pursuing grants to support local housing development goals.

items.headline: City seeking public input on $2026-27 housing assistance spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting public comments on how it will spend federal HUD grant funds for housing and urban development in the upcoming fiscal year. This is your chance to weigh in on housing priorities before the plan is finalized.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting to discuss its safe parking initiative, which typically provides designated lots for people living in vehicles. This is a chance for residents to learn about or weigh in on the program's scope and implementation.
